ogTitle: Lane Cove North vs Lane Cove West: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lane Cove North and Lane Cove West.
heading: Lane Cove North vs Lane Cove West — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lane Cove North, New South Wales and Lane Cove West, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lane Cove North has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Lane Cove West (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lane Cove North or Lane Cove West safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lane Cove North has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Lane Cove West scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lane Cove North scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Lane Cove West scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lane Cove North or Lane Cove West?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lane Cove North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
